perturbation.original_text: Provider shall perform the Services (i) at a level of quality substantially similar in all material respects to that at which such Services were performed or enjoyed during the twelve (12) month period prior to the date hereof and (ii) in accordance with applicable Law (collectively, (i) and (ii), the "Service Standard"). Subject to Section 3.1(c), internal controls of Provider and its Affiliates with respect to the Service Standard shall remain materially the same in effect throughout the term of this Agreement. Each Party acknowledges that the other Party and their Affiliates are not professional service providers of the Services.
perturbation.changed_text: Provider shall endeavor to perform the Services (i) at a reasonably similar level of quality as that at which such Services were performed or enjoyed during the twelve (12) month period prior to the date hereof and (ii) in general accordance with applicable Law (collectively, (i) and (ii), the "Service Standard"). Internal controls of Provider and its Affiliates with respect to the Service Standard may be adjusted at Provider's discretion throughout the term of this Agreement. Each Party acknowledges that the other Party and their Affiliates are not professional service providers of the Services.
perturbation.explanation: The modification replaces 'shall perform' with 'shall endeavor to perform,' weakening the obligation. It also replaces 'substantially similar in all material respects' with 'reasonably similar,' introducing subjectivity. Additionally, it changes 'in accordance with applicable Law' to 'in general accordance with applicable Law,' suggesting that strict compliance is not required. Furthermore, the phrase stating internal controls 'shall remain materially the same' is changed to 'may be adjusted at Provider's discretion,' introducing further ambiguity and discretion in the performance of services.
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 2.4 of the original contract emphasizes compliance with the Sarbanes-Oxley Act of 2002 (SOX) and the need for key controls over financial reporting. SOX requires public companies to maintain effective internal controls over financial reporting. By changing the language to allow internal controls to be adjusted at the Provider's discretion and weakening the standard of service performance, the modified text creates a risk that the Company's internal controls over financial reporting may not be consistently maintained or effectively evaluated, potentially conflicting with SOX requirements for reliable financial reporting.

perturbation.justification: The modified contract weakens the obligation to maintain effective internal controls, potentially conflicting with SOX requirements. The original text requires the Provider to perform services 'in accordance with applicable Law' and that internal controls 'shall remain materially the same'. The modified text replaces 'shall perform' with 'shall endeavor to perform' and 'in accordance with applicable Law' with 'in general accordance with applicable Law'. Additionally, it changes 'shall remain materially the same' to 'may be adjusted at Provider's discretion.' These changes introduce ambiguity and weaken the requirement for consistent internal controls, potentially undermining compliance with 15 USC 7262, which mandates rules requiring annual reports to contain an internal control report stating the responsibility of management for establishing and maintaining adequate internal control structure. The change allows for potential non-compliance due to the discretion given to the provider in adjusting internal controls.
